comment.body:
at zero vanna, from what I understand it's when delta changes the least amount with respect to IV/volatility in general. would this not usually lead to a "stickiness" in price like what Alma referred to earlier this morning? because it makes it a lot easier to delta hedge at these levels with respect to vol? adding to GEX being balanced there as well? 

Charm towards EOD would impact this with delta decay though? or am I interpreting/extrapolating that incorrectly?

comment.body:
it is refer to the zero speed line and price movements... ·       above zero speed, MMs are longerskew: longer spot/vol beta, shorter upside gamma/longer downside gamma
·       below zero speed, MMs are shorterskew: shorter spot/vol beta, longer upside gamma/shorter downside gamma.
comment.raw_body:

comment.body:
Alma, I was wondering if you would be interested in updating the Reversion Threshold script so that the levels are added where appropriate depending on which ticker you're seeing. I mean: If you're looking at SPX, SPX levels are shown. If you're looking at SPY, SPY levels are shown, if you're looking at ES, ES levels are shown.

This way, we could copy-paste all the levels you share into the pine editor each day and do not have to copy-paste for each ticker if we jump from one ticker to another.

I have a script that does this, so I could share it with you if you'd like to modify yours. If not, that's fine too :)

comment.body:
The way I made it work in my case was:
1. Copy paste code in editor and save as new Indicator - IMPORTANT: When saving or adding to a chart it will ask you for the input, just skip that part/leave it empty
2. When the indicator is loaded in the chart, click on settings and in the input section copy paste the long string for the corresponding ticker.
3. It has been perfectly working for me since then, using it for SPX and ES
comment.raw_body:
The way I made it work in my case was:

2. When the indicator is loaded in the chart, click on settings and in the input section copy paste the long string for the corresponding ticker.
3. It has been perfectly working for me since then, using it for SPX and ES
